items.title: FUE vs DHI: Which Hair Transplant Technique is Right for You?
items.body:
When researching hair transplantation, you will quickly encounter two dominant techniques: FUE (Follicular Unit Extraction) and DHI (Direct Hair Implantation). Both deliver natural, permanent results — but they work differently and suit different patients.

What is FUE?

FUE involves extracting individual hair follicles from the donor area (typically the back of the scalp) using a micro-punch tool, then creating tiny channels in the recipient area before implanting the grafts. It is the most widely performed hair transplant technique globally.

FUE is ideal for patients needing 2,000–, those who want to keep their hair short, and anyone undergoing their first transplant. Recovery is 3–5 days back to normal activity.

What is DHI?

DHI uses the Choi implanter pen to extract and implant follicles simultaneously — eliminating the channel-opening step. This reduces the time grafts spend outside the body, increasing survival rate, and allows for greater control over angle and direction of growth.

DHI is ideal for patients who want maximum density, those adding to existing hair, or advanced cases (Norwood V–VII). Healing is typically faster than standard FUE.

Which should you choose?

  • Large area, first transplant → FUE
  • Maximum density, advanced loss → DHI
  • Adding to existing hair → DHI
  • Tight budget → FUE (slightly lower cost)
  • Premium result, less trauma → Sapphire FUE

The honest answer: both techniques in expert hands deliver excellent results. Your surgeon will recommend the best option after examining your donor area and hair loss pattern.

How does Nevora decide?

We analyse your photos via WhatsApp before you travel. Our surgeons assess donor density, scalp laxity and hair loss stage to recommend the optimal technique. There is no upselling — we recommend what will give you the best result.

items.title: Hair Transplant in Istanbul: The Complete Guide for Arab Patients
items.body:
Istanbul has become the global capital of hair transplantation, and for good reason. The city offers world-class surgical expertise, internationally trained teams, and all-inclusive packages at a fraction of the cost of the Gulf, UK or Europe. For Arab patients, there is an added advantage: a large Arabic-speaking medical community and strong cultural familiarity.

Why Istanbul?

Turkey performs more hair transplants per year than any other country. The combination of highly trained surgeons, competitive pricing due to currency exchange rates, and luxury hospitality infrastructure makes Istanbul the logical choice for medical tourists from the Arab world.

A procedure costing €8,000–€15,000 in the UK or Germany typically costs €2,000–€5,000 in Istanbul — with no compromise in quality at reputable clinics.

Flying from Kuwait, UAE or Saudi Arabia

Direct flights to Istanbul from Kuwait City, Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Riyadh and Jeddah run daily. Flight time is 3–4 hours. Visa requirements for GCC nationals are typically straightforward — check current requirements with the Turkish embassy before booking.

What to expect on arrival

  • Your clinic coordinator meets you at Istanbul Airport
  • Transfer to your hotel in an air-conditioned private vehicle
  • Pre-procedure consultation and blood tests (same day or next morning)
  • Procedure day: typically 6–8 hours including preparation and recovery
  • Post-op check the following morning before your return flight

Language and culture

At Nevora Istanbul, our coordination team includes Arabic-speaking staff who manage your entire journey from your first WhatsApp message to your 12-month follow-up. Halal food options are available at your hotel, and prayer times are respected throughout your stay.

Booking checklist

  • Send WhatsApp photos for a free pre-assessment
  • Confirm your graft count and technique with the surgeon
  • Book flights and inform your coordinator of arrival time
  • Stop blood thinners and supplements 1 week before (if applicable)
  • Arrange 2–3 days post-procedure before flying home

items.title: Month-by-Month: What to Expect After Your Hair Transplant
items.body:
One of the most important things to understand before a hair transplant is that the result you see at 1 month is not the final result. Hair transplantation follows a predictable biological timeline, and knowing what to expect prevents unnecessary anxiety.

Days 1–14: The initial phase

Immediately after the procedure, tiny scabs form around each implanted graft. The transplanted area will appear red and there may be minor swelling around the forehead. This is completely normal. You will wash your hair gently with the provided shampoo from day 3.

Do not touch, scratch or expose the transplanted area to direct sunlight for the first 14 days. Sleep with your head elevated at 45 degrees for the first week.

Weeks 2–4: Scabs fall, hair follows

The scabs will naturally shed by day 10–14. Many patients are alarmed to find that the transplanted hairs fall out with the scabs. This is completely normal and expected — it is called "shock loss."

Months 1–3: The quiet phase

This is the most psychologically challenging period. The transplanted follicles enter a resting phase. The scalp looks similar to before the procedure. Patients often worry the transplant has not worked. It has — the follicles are alive underground, preparing to grow.

Months 3–6: First signs of growth

At around 3 months, fine new hairs begin to emerge. By month 5–6, coverage is visible and encouraging. The hairs are initially thin and slightly wavy — they will thicken and straighten over the following months.

Months 6–12: Transformation

This is the exciting phase. Hair thickens, coverage fills in dramatically, and by month 10–12, the final result is clear. Most patients are fully satisfied with their results at this point.

Full, permanent results are visible at 12 months. For patients over 45 or with very dense implantation, the final result may mature until month 14–18.

items.title: Why Does Hair Transplant Cost So Much Less in Turkey?
items.body:
A question we hear from every new patient: "The quality looks identical to clinics in the UK or Germany. Why is it so much cheaper in Istanbul?" It is a fair question, and the answer is not what most people assume.

It is not lower quality — it is economics

The cost difference is almost entirely explained by currency exchange rates, lower operational costs and higher procedure volumes — not any difference in surgeon training, technology or materials used.

Turkish surgeons often trained in the same institutions as their European counterparts. Many have performed more procedures due to Istanbul's higher volume — which means more experience, not less quality.

The numbers in context

  • UK / Germany: €8,000 – €15,000 for
  • UAE / Kuwait private clinics: €6,000 – €12,000
  • Istanbul (reputable clinic): €2,000 – €5,000 all-inclusive

What drives the price difference?

  • Lower staff wages relative to Western Europe
  • Lower clinic rent and operational overhead
  • Favourable Turkish lira exchange rate for foreign patients
  • Higher volume = lower cost per procedure
  • Government support for medical tourism infrastructure

What to watch out for

The low-cost segment does exist and should be avoided. Clinics offering procedures under €1,000 often use untrained technicians rather than surgeons. Always verify: is your procedure performed by a qualified surgeon? What is the surgeon's graft count track record? Does the clinic have JCI or ISO certification?

At Nevora Istanbul, all procedures are performed by certified surgeons. We are happy to share our credentials, surgeon profiles and patient outcomes during your free consultation.

items.title: What is the Norwood Scale and What Stage Are You?
items.body:
The Norwood-Hamilton Scale is the internationally recognised classification system for male pattern baldness. First published in 1951 and refined in 1975, it divides hair loss into 7 stages — and it is the foundation of every hair transplant consultation.

The 7 Norwood Stages

  • Stage I: No significant hair loss. Slight recession at temples possible.
  • Stage II: Slight recession at temples. Often called a "mature hairline."
  • Stage III: Deeper recession at temples creating an M or U shape. The first stage typically recommended for transplantation.
  • Stage IV: Significant hairline recession plus thinning at the crown. More pronounced M shape.
  • Stage V: Large areas of loss at both front and crown, separated by a narrow band of hair.
  • Stage VI: Front and crown merge into one large area of baldness.
  • Stage VII: Only a narrow horseshoe of hair remains at the sides and back.
Most hair transplant patients are Stage III to Stage V. Stages VI and VII require careful planning to achieve good coverage using available donor hair — experienced surgeons manage this well.

Why does it matter for your transplant?

Your Norwood stage determines your graft requirement. Stage III typically needs 1,500–. Stage V may need 4,000–5,000. It also influences technique selection — DHI is preferred for Stage V and above.

How do I find out my stage?

Send a top-down photo and a front-facing photo via WhatsApp. Our team will classify your stage and recommend graft count and technique — free, with no obligation.

items.title: PRP Therapy After Hair Transplant: Is It Worth It?
items.body:
Platelet-Rich Plasma (PRP) therapy has become one of the most discussed add-ons in hair restoration. Supporters claim it accelerates healing and boosts growth. Sceptics argue the evidence is mixed. Here is a balanced look at what the research actually shows — and who should consider it.

What is PRP?

PRP involves drawing a small amount of your own blood, spinning it in a centrifuge to concentrate the platelets (growth factors), then injecting the concentrate into the scalp. Because it uses your own blood, there is zero risk of allergic reaction or rejection.

What does the evidence say?

Multiple clinical studies have shown that PRP applied at the time of hair transplant improves graft survival and accelerates the early growth phase. One widely cited study found patients who received PRP alongside FUE had measurably denser results at 6 months compared to FUE alone.

PRP is not a substitute for hair transplantation — it is a complementary treatment that can enhance and accelerate the result. On its own, PRP may slow further hair loss but will not replace lost hair.

Who benefits most?

  • Patients with borderline donor density who need every graft to survive
  • Anyone wanting to accelerate the visible result timeline
  • Patients with active thinning who want to preserve existing hair
  • Older patients (40+) who may have slower healing

How many sessions?

The standard protocol is 3 sessions: one at the time of transplant, one at 1 month, and one at 3 months. Some surgeons recommend a maintenance session at 6 months for patients with ongoing hair loss.

Our recommendation

At Nevora Istanbul, PRP is included in our Comfort and Elite packages. For patients choosing the Essential package with significant hair loss or over 35 years old, we recommend adding it. The cost is modest relative to the potential benefit.

items.title: Donor Area: Why It Determines Everything About Your Result
items.body:
Before any consultation, any AI analysis, any technique discussion — the single most important question about your hair transplant is this: what does your donor area look like? Everything else follows from the answer.

What is the donor area?

The donor area is the strip of hair at the back and sides of your scalp — typically between the ears, around the occipital bone. This hair is genetically resistant to the hormone DHT, which means it will not fall out even after transplantation. It is permanent by nature.

What makes a good donor area?

  • High follicle density (more grafts available per cm²)
  • Coarse, thick hair shaft (covers more scalp per graft)
  • Low scalp miniaturisation (healthy, active follicles)
  • Good scalp laxity (looser scalp = easier extraction)
A patient with high donor density and coarse hair may achieve excellent coverage with . A patient with fine, sparse donor hair may need for a similar result. Donor quality is not negotiable — it is what it is.

Can anything improve donor quality?

No treatment permanently increases donor density. However, PRP therapy before the procedure can strengthen existing follicles and improve extraction yield. Minoxidil applied to the donor area for 3 months pre-procedure has also shown benefit in some studies.

What if my donor area is limited?

Limited donor supply requires careful planning. An experienced surgeon will map your donor zone precisely, extract conservatively to avoid visible thinning, and prioritise the frontal hairline and crown strategically. Beard hair can also be used as a supplementary donor source in advanced cases.

How do we assess your donor area?

Send us a top-down photo and a photo of the back of your head via WhatsApp. Our team will estimate your available grafts and advise on realistic coverage expectations — free, with no obligation.

items.title: Sapphire FUE vs Standard FUE: Is the Upgrade Worth It?
items.body:
Standard FUE uses steel micro-punch tools. Sapphire FUE uses blades made from synthetic sapphire crystal. It sounds like a marketing upgrade — but the difference is real, and for the right patient, it matters.

What is the actual difference?

In standard FUE, channels (the tiny incisions where grafts are placed) are created with a steel blade. Sapphire blades are harder, sharper and smoother than steel. They create finer channels with less tissue damage on each pass.

The smoother the channel, the less inflammation. Less inflammation means faster healing, reduced crust formation, and in dense implantations, a slightly higher graft survival rate.

Who benefits most from Sapphire FUE?

  • Patients requiring high-density implantation ( in a concentrated area)
  • Anyone with sensitive skin or a history of slow healing
  • Patients who need to return to normal life quickly (e.g. back to work within 3 days)
  • Those with fine hair who need maximum graft survival per follicle

Is the result different long-term?

At 12 months, a standard FUE and Sapphire FUE performed by the same skilled surgeon on two comparable patients will look extremely similar. The primary advantages of sapphire are in the recovery phase and in very dense implantation zones.

Our recommendation

At Nevora Istanbul, Sapphire FUE is included in our Comfort and Elite packages and is our standard for dense-zone implantation. For most patients, it is the right choice. Your surgeon will confirm technique selection during your free consultation.

items.title: What to Eat After a Hair Transplant to Speed Up Growth
items.body:
Hair is one of the fastest-growing tissues in the body — but it is also the lowest priority when your body distributes nutrients. After a hair transplant, what you eat directly influences how quickly grafts establish and how fast new hair emerges.

The key nutrients for hair growth

  • Protein: Hair is made of keratin — a protein. Inadequate protein intake slows new hair growth significantly. Target 1.2–1.5g per kg of body weight per day.
  • Biotin (B7): Supports keratin production. Found in eggs, nuts, and legumes. Deficiency is rare but supplementation is harmless.
  • Iron: Essential for carrying oxygen to hair follicles. Deficiency is one of the most common causes of slow post-transplant growth.
  • Zinc: Supports follicle repair and protein synthesis. Found in meat, shellfish, and seeds.
  • Vitamin D: Low levels are strongly associated with hair loss. Supplement if your levels are below optimal.
The most impactful nutritional intervention is simply eating adequate protein. This single factor makes a measurable difference to growth speed. Aim for eggs, chicken, fish, legumes or quality protein supplementation daily.

What to avoid

  • Alcohol: Reduces blood flow to the scalp and impairs zinc absorption. Avoid for at least 1 month post-procedure.
  • Crash dieting: Caloric restriction signals your body to redirect energy away from hair follicles.
  • Excess sugar: Promotes inflammation which can slow healing in the recipient area.

Our post-op nutrition guide

Every Nevora patient receives a detailed nutrition and lifestyle guide covering the first 12 months. It is part of our commitment to supporting your result long after you leave Istanbul.

items.title: Hair Transplant Recovery: Week-by-Week Timeline
items.body:
One of the most common questions patients ask before their hair transplant is: "What will recovery actually look like?" Here is a realistic, week-by-week guide to what you can expect after your procedure at Nevora Istanbul.

Days 1–3: The First 72 Hours

Immediately after the procedure, the transplanted area will appear red and small scabs will begin forming around each follicle. This is completely normal and expected. You may experience mild swelling across the forehead — this typically peaks around day 2 and resolves within 5 days.

Sleep with your head elevated at 45 degrees for the first three nights. This reduces swelling and protects the newly implanted follicles.

Week 1: Scab Formation

During the first week, scabs will form and harden around each implanted follicle. Do not pick, scratch or rub the area. Your medical team will provide a gentle saline spray and a specific washing technique to keep the area clean without disturbing the grafts.

Week 2: The Scabs Fall Away

By the end of the second week, most scabs will have naturally shed. The transplanted area will look pink and the donor area will have largely healed. You can resume light daily activities.

Weeks 3–6: Shock Loss — Do Not Panic

This is the phase that surprises most patients. The transplanted hairs will shed — this is called shock loss and it is a completely normal and necessary phase of the growth cycle. The follicle itself remains alive beneath the scalp.

Shock loss does not mean the transplant has failed. It is a sign the follicle is entering a new growth phase. This is the most important thing to understand about hair transplant recovery.

Months 3–4: The First New Growth

Around month 3, you will begin to notice fine, soft new hairs emerging. Growth at this stage is uneven and the texture may differ from your natural hair — this is temporary.

Months 6–9: Visible Transformation

By month 6, the transformation becomes clearly visible. Hair gains thickness, texture normalises and the hairline takes its final shape. Most patients are thrilled with results at this stage.

Month 12: Your Final Result

Full, mature results are typically visible at 12 months. The transplanted hair is permanent — it will continue to grow, grey and behave exactly like your natural hair for the rest of your life.

items.title: Hair Transplant Cost: Why Turkey Offers the Best Value in the World
items.body:
If you have been researching hair transplants, you have probably noticed a dramatic difference in pricing between the UK, Europe, the Gulf and Turkey. This guide explains why — and what to look for to ensure you are comparing like with like.

Average Costs by Country

In the UK, a hair transplant typically costs between £8,000 and £15,000. In Germany and France, prices range from €7,000 to €13,000. Clinics in the UAE charge between AED 25,000 and AED 50,000. In Turkey, the same procedure with the same quality surgical team costs a fraction of this — typically between €2,500 and €5,000 all-inclusive.

The price difference is not due to lower quality. It reflects Turkey's lower cost of living, the exchange rate, and a highly competitive medical tourism market that has invested heavily in clinical infrastructure.

What Should Be Included in the Price?

When comparing clinics, always check what is included. A genuine all-inclusive package should cover the procedure itself, hospital or clinic fees, local anaesthesia, all medications, post-operative care kit, airport transfers and optional hotel arrangements if required.

What Drives the Cost in Turkey?

Turkey has over two decades of specialised hair transplant experience and has produced some of the world's most skilled hair restoration surgeons. The country's infrastructure — from dedicated hair clinics to international airports — has been built around medical tourism.

Hidden Costs to Watch For

Be cautious of clinics that advertise very low base prices and then add charges for the number of grafts, use of the Choi pen (DHI), or PRP treatment. At Nevora, our packages are transparent and all-inclusive from the first consultation.

Is Turkey Safe for Medical Travel?

Istanbul is one of the world's most visited cities and is fully equipped for international patients. Modern clinics hold international accreditations, surgeons are board-certified, and the quality of care meets or exceeds European standards.

items.title: What Causes Hair Loss? The Complete Medical Guide
items.body:
Hair loss affects millions of men globally, yet the underlying causes are often misunderstood. Understanding why hair loss occurs is the first step to addressing it effectively.

Male Pattern Baldness: The Primary Cause

Androgenetic alopecia — commonly known as male pattern baldness — accounts for approximately 95% of hair loss in men. It is driven by a genetic sensitivity to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone derived from testosterone. DHT causes hair follicles to shrink progressively, producing thinner and shorter hairs until the follicle eventually stops producing hair altogether.

Hair transplantation works because the follicles taken from the donor area (typically the back and sides of the scalp) are genetically resistant to DHT. When transplanted, they retain this resistance and continue to grow for life.

The Norwood Scale

The Norwood Scale is the standard classification system for measuring the extent of male pattern baldness, ranging from Type 1 (no significant loss) to Type 7 (extensive loss across the entire top of the scalp). Hair transplantation is suitable for most Norwood types, with the approach tailored to the individual's specific pattern and donor availability.

Other Causes of Hair Loss

While androgenetic alopecia is most common, hair loss can also be caused by nutritional deficiencies (particularly iron, zinc and vitamin D), chronic stress, thyroid disorders, certain medications, and autoimmune conditions such as alopecia areata. A thorough consultation will assess whether your hair loss is androgenetic or secondary to another cause.

When Is the Right Time for a Hair Transplant?

Most specialists recommend waiting until hair loss has stabilised — typically from the mid-twenties onwards. Transplanting too early can result in continued natural hair loss around the transplanted area, requiring further procedures later.

items.title: How to Prepare for Your Hair Transplant: The Complete Pre-Op Guide
items.body:
The success of your hair transplant begins well before you arrive in Istanbul. Proper preparation improves graft survival, reduces the risk of complications and ensures a smoother recovery.

Two Weeks Before: Stop Blood Thinners

If you take aspirin, ibuprofen, or any anticoagulant medication, you will need to stop these at least two weeks before your procedure. These medications increase bleeding during surgery, which can affect graft placement. Always consult your GP before stopping any prescribed medication.

One Week Before: Avoid Alcohol

Alcohol dilates blood vessels and increases bleeding. Avoid alcohol entirely for at least one week before your procedure. It also dehydrates the body and can affect anaesthesia.

Do not cut or shave your hair before the procedure unless specifically instructed by your clinic. In most cases, the surgical team will trim the donor area on the day of the procedure.

The Night Before: Rest and Hydrate

Eat a nutritious meal the evening before and ensure you are well hydrated. Get a full night of sleep. Wear comfortable, loose-fitting clothing on the day — ideally a button-up shirt or zip-up top so you do not need to pull anything over your head afterwards.

Morning of the Procedure

Wash your hair thoroughly with your normal shampoo but do not apply any styling products. Eat a light breakfast — you will not be under general anaesthesia, so you do not need to fast. Arrive at the clinic calmly; the procedure is straightforward and the vast majority of patients are surprised by how comfortable it is.

What to Bring to Istanbul

Pack loose, button-up shirts for the days following the procedure. Bring a soft travel pillow for your return flight. Avoid tight-fitting hats or caps. Your Nevora team will provide a full post-operative kit including specialist shampoo, saline spray and all necessary medications.

items.title: Sapphire FUE vs Standard FUE: What Is the Difference?
items.body:
Sapphire FUE has emerged as one of the most talked-about refinements in hair transplant surgery. But what exactly is it, and is it right for you?

The Core Difference: The Blade Material

Standard FUE uses steel micro-blades to create the recipient channels — the tiny incisions into which the harvested follicles are placed. Sapphire FUE uses blades made from synthetic sapphire, a material that is significantly harder, smoother and more precise than steel.

Why Does the Blade Material Matter?

The quality of the recipient channels directly affects the angle, direction and density of the transplanted hairs. Sapphire blades create smoother, more consistent channels with less tissue trauma. This translates to reduced post-operative swelling, faster healing and — critically — a more natural-looking final result.

Sapphire blades allow surgeons to create channels as small as 1.0–1.5mm, compared to 1.5–2.0mm with standard steel. This precision enables higher density in the same surface area.

Who Is Sapphire FUE Best Suited For?

Sapphire FUE is particularly beneficial for patients with sensitive scalps, those seeking maximum density in the hairline area, and patients undergoing a second procedure. It is the default technique at Nevora for most procedures.

Is Sapphire FUE Worth It?

For patients who want the most refined, least traumatic procedure with the most natural results, Sapphire FUE represents the current gold standard. The additional precision it offers is particularly valuable in the frontal hairline — the most visible and aesthetically critical zone.

items.title: Hair Transplant Aftercare: The First 30 Days
items.body:
The first 30 days following your hair transplant are critical. Proper aftercare protects your investment and maximises the number of grafts that successfully establish and grow.

Days 1–5: Protect the Grafts

The transplanted follicles are not yet anchored firmly in place during the first five days. Avoid any direct contact with the recipient area. Do not touch, scratch or rub the area. Sleep on your back with your head elevated. Avoid bending forward or any activity that increases blood pressure to the head.

The first wash is typically performed on day 3 or 4 using a specific technique your clinic will demonstrate. Use only the specialist shampoo provided — standard shampoos contain detergents and fragrances that can irritate the healing scalp.

Days 5–10: Gentle Washing Routine

From day 5, you can begin a gentle daily washing routine. Apply a small amount of specialist shampoo to the palm of your hand, lather gently, and let it run over the transplanted area without rubbing. Rinse with lukewarm (not hot) water from a low-pressure setting.

Week 2: Return to Light Activity

After 10 days, most patients can return to light daily activities and office work. Continue to avoid direct sunlight on the scalp, dusty environments and strenuous exercise. The donor area will have largely healed by this point.

Weeks 3–4: Maintenance Phase

Continue the gentle washing routine. The scabs will have shed and the area may look temporarily sparse as shock loss begins. This is the phase that tests patience — trust the process. The follicle is alive and will begin producing new hair growth within weeks.

What to Avoid in the First 30 Days

Swimming (pool chlorine and sea salt both irritate the healing scalp), direct sun exposure without SPF protection, wearing tight hats or helmets, hair colouring or chemical treatments, alcohol and smoking (both impair blood supply to the scalp), and vigorous exercise that causes heavy sweating.

items.title: Hair Transplant in Istanbul: The Complete Guide for International Patients
items.body:
Istanbul has established itself as the global capital of hair transplantation, performing more procedures annually than any other city in the world. For patients travelling from the Gulf, UK, Europe and beyond, this guide covers everything you need to know.

Why Do Patients Choose Istanbul?

Three factors have made Istanbul the world's leading destination for hair restoration: surgical expertise built over two decades, significantly lower costs compared to the UK and Europe, and a world-class medical infrastructure combined with one of Europe's most compelling cities to visit.

How to Choose a Clinic

With hundreds of clinics operating in Istanbul, selecting the right one requires careful research. Look for clinics where procedures are performed by board-certified surgeons — not unlicensed technicians, which remains a genuine concern in the industry. Ask to see before-and-after photos of actual patients, request to speak with a coordinator fluent in your language, and verify the clinic's accreditations.

A key question to ask any clinic: will a surgeon perform my procedure, or will it be delegated to technicians? At Nevora, every procedure is surgeon-led from extraction to implantation.

What to Expect on the Day

Your procedure day at Nevora begins with a pre-operative consultation and hairline design with your surgeon. Local anaesthesia is administered — this is the only moment of discomfort in the entire procedure. The extraction and implantation typically take 6–8 hours. Most patients watch films, listen to music or sleep through much of the procedure.

Getting to Istanbul

Istanbul is served by two international airports — Istanbul Airport (IST) and Sabiha Gökçen (SAW). Direct flights operate from most major Gulf cities, London, Paris, Frankfurt and across Europe. Nevora provides private VIP airport transfers for all patients.

The Istanbul Experience

Most patients spend 3–5 days in Istanbul. After the procedure, the first two days call for rest. By day 3, many patients comfortably explore the city — from the historic Hagia Sophia and Grand Bazaar in Sultanahmet to the boutiques and restaurants of Nişantaşı. It is an unexpectedly enjoyable experience.
